$comment: engine.mcp.v1 — the ENGINE BOX's own self-heal control surface (ADR-060 §7 baseline; Talk lane handoff item 1). SEPARATE from control.mcp.v1 (the desktop app's health) and hands.mcp.v1 (drives OTHER apps): this is the health of the voice-engine box (the 5090 running engine/server.py — STT + TTS + brain-stem). Before this, a wedged engine box could only be FALLEN AWAY FROM (control.mcp.v1 set_engine_url) — never HEALED. Now an agent can diagnose it and restart the worker remotely. Two-layer / doctor-not-in-patient per docs/CONTROL_SURFACE_DESIGN: the surface is hosted by a SUPERVISOR process (server/supervise.py) that owns the engine WORKER as a subprocess, so restart_engine works even when the worker is hung — the doctor is not in the patient. Bilingual (MCP + native HTTP /tools /invoke /mcp) per ADR-060 §3.2, same security wall (per-install token + loopback bind + Origin/Host reject). Change control: additive -> v1.1 via PR; breaking -> v2 + tell Grant.
$comment_class: FLAG for the doctrine lane: class is 'cloud' (the engine box is a server/service, not a user desktop), but the security wall is desktop-style (per-install token + loopback bind) because the surface binds loopback ON the box and is reached over SSH tunnel / the server/ relay — NOT the ADR-060 §3.3 cloud EPT+CONFIRM_FLOW. Flagged to Grant: ratify loopback-token for an on-box server surface, or require EPT.

security.$comment: Per-install bearer token (constant-time compare), loopback-only bind on the engine box, reject any request carrying an Origin header, no CORS. Remote reach is via SSH tunnel or the server/ relay (entitlement-gated) — never a public bind. Same proven wall as hands/surface.py + control.mcp.v1.

tiers.$comment: Enforced at the surface (server/supervise.py), not the caller. auto_allow: execute immediately. ask_first: per-invocation confirmation. always_confirm: per-invocation confirmation, no session upgrade. A denial returns ok:false error:'denied' — never silence.

platform_note: Tools that this v1 does not yet implement return ok:false error:'unsupported' with an honest reason (tri-state per ADR-060) — the tool never disappears from the list, so a future agent finds it and gets 'not wired, here's why' rather than 'unknown tool'. get_capabilities reports each tool true / false / 'unsupported'.

tools.name: get_health
tools.description: The FIRST tool when the voice engine seems down. One call → is the engine box healthy: worker process alive, providers (STT/TTS) warm, GPU present, websocket serving, and not crash-looping.

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Current engine runtime state: serving/warming/restarting/down, active session count, and uptime.

tools.name: get_config
tools.description: The engine's effective configuration: selected STT / TTS / brain providers, compute device, and bind port. Secrets redacted.

tools.name: get_logs
tools.description: Recent engine-box logs, scrubbed of secrets and user content. For diagnosing a wedge or crash-loop.

tools.name: run_selftest
tools.description: Actively exercise the engine's core path on the box (engine/pipeline.py): synthesize a phrase → transcribe it → confirm round-trip, with per-stage pass/fail + timings. Proof the STT and TTS providers actually work here, not just that the process is up.

tools.name: get_capabilities
tools.description: Which engine control tools actually work on this box right now — tri-state true / false / 'unsupported' per tool (ADR-060). Honest about what this v1 surface can and can't heal.

tools.name: reconnect
tools.description: Re-warm the engine's providers and re-establish the upstream brain (Mind) connection WITHOUT restarting the worker process — the lightest heal. Blocks up to ~15 s.

returns.$comment: human-readable outcome; failures surface as ok:false error.

tools.name: restart_engine
tools.description: THE core heal: the supervisor restarts the engine WORKER process. Works even when the worker is hung (the supervisor owns it — doctor-not-in-patient), so this recovers a wedged engine that reconnect can't. Active voice sessions drop and reconnect. Response is sent BEFORE the restart executes (response-ordering).

returns.$comment: e.g. 'restarting the engine worker'; failures surface as ok:false error.

tools.name: enter_safe_mode
tools.description: Drop the engine to its most reliable minimal provider set (smallest STT, CPU device, default TTS) to keep SOME service when the full stack is failing. Persisted so a crash-loop restarts into safe mode.

returns.$comment: Declared; tri-state 'unsupported' in v1 until the minimal-provider path is wired.

tools.name: exit_safe_mode
tools.description: Leave safe mode and restore the full provider set.

returns.$comment: Declared; tri-state 'unsupported' in v1 (pairs with enter_safe_mode).

tools.name: set_engine_config
tools.description: Change the engine's providers/device (e.g. switch STT model, move to CPU) via a validated config, applied on the next worker restart. The engine's typed settings catalog.

returns.$comment: Declared; tri-state 'unsupported' in v1 until the persisted engine-config store + undo lands.

tools.name: check_for_update
tools.description: Is a newer engine-box deployment available to install? A read-only check that reports the current and latest engine versions.

returns.$comment: Declared; tri-state 'unsupported' in v1 (no engine update pipeline yet).

tools.name: apply_update
tools.description: Deploy a pending engine update and restart the worker. Attested + rollback when wired.

returns.$comment: Declared; tri-state 'unsupported' in v1 (the engine self-update pipeline is a follow-up).

tools.name: reset_to_defaults
tools.description: Reset the engine to its default provider set and restart the worker. The big red button for a mis-configured engine box.

returns.$comment: e.g. 'reset to defaults; restarting the worker'.

$tool_count_note: 14 tools: 6 diagnostics (get_health, get_status, get_config, get_logs, run_selftest, get_capabilities) + 8 recovery/config (reconnect, restart_engine, enter_safe_mode, exit_safe_mode, set_engine_config, check_for_update, apply_update, reset_to_defaults). v1 implements the diagnostics + reconnect + restart_engine + reset_to_defaults LIVE; enter/exit_safe_mode, set_engine_config, check_for_update, apply_update are declared but tri-state 'unsupported' until wired.
